components.name: dipotassium hydrogen phosphate (K2HPO4)
components.evidence: DBM medium contained 3.88 g L−1 K2HPO4

components.name: magnesium chloride hexahydrate (MgCl2·6H2O)
components.evidence: 0.1 g L−1 MgCl2∙6H2O, with the initial pH adjusted at 7.0

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: All bacterial cultivations were performed in de Bont minimal medium containing 20 mM glucose.

components.name: EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid)
components.evidence: trace element solution [10 mg L−1 ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA)

media.base_medium: de Bont minimal medium (DBM), Hartmans/de Bont mineral medium

sources_chased.what: base medium cited as ref 34 (Hartmans, Smits, van der Werf, Volkering, de Bont 1989, Appl Environ Microbiol 55:2850-2855, PMC203180, doi:10.1128/aem.55.11.2850-2855.1989)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch of PMC203180 HTML and PDF returned only abstract/download-shell (methods not text-extractable); full DBM recipe including complete trace-element solution is already spelled out verbatim in the main text of PMC10308882, so no additional constituents were recoverable or needed

media.notes: The paper's main text gives the DBM recipe in full: phosphate buffer (K2HPO4 + NaH2PO4), (NH4)2SO4 as N/S source, MgCl2·6H2O, and the complete de Bont trace-element solution (EDTA chelator + Zn, Ca, Fe, Mo, Cu, Co, Mn salts). DBM is a defined medium requiring no vitamins/growth factors for P. putida; none are added. Standard carbon source is 20 mM glucose (methanol and formate are experiment-specific supplements, not part of the base medium). Cultivations are aerobic (P. putida is an obligate aerobe). All element exchanges in current_components are covered; concentrations and exact salt identities were added. EDTA is a chelator, not a modeled nutrient exchange.
